title: Oreas involucrata
taxonomy: Oreas involucrata is classified within the plant family Asteraceae, which includes daisies and sunflowers. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. Specifically, Oreas involucrata is often placed within the subfamily Asteroideae. intro: Oreas involucrata, also known as Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and antidiarrheal properties, making it a natural remedy for urinary tract infections and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Oreas involucrata include arbutin, a glycoside responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Bearberry is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is used by herbalists to support urinary health and prevent kidney stones.
